interpretation_en: A sign of the well and constant supply—the fundamental resources that sustain life. This represents the source of nourishment and support. One should cultivate and maintain this source.

name: 革
judgment: 革：巳日乃孚，元亨。利贞。悔亡。
tuanshu: 革，水火相息，二女同居，其志不相得，曰革。巳日乃孚；革而信也。文明以说，大亨以正，革而当，其悔乃亡。天地革而四时成，汤武革命，顺乎天而应乎人，革之时义大矣哉！
image:
泽中有火，革；君子以治历明时。
巩用黄牛，不可以有为也。
巳日革之，行有嘉也。
革言三就，又何之矣。
改命之吉，信志也。
大人虎变，其文炳也。
君子豹变，其文蔚也。小人革面，顺以从君也。
interpretation: 泽火相革、变革之象。问事多涉变动（制度、人事、关系）。但变革需'己日乃孚'——时机成熟、众信之后方可行。
vernacular_judgment: 巳日乃孚，元亨，利贞，悔亡。
vernacular_image: 泽中有火，革，君子以治历明时。
judgment_en: Revolution. If the great person asks, change. Small matters should not be changed.
image_en:
Lake above, fire below—this is Revolution;
The nobleman announces the punishment in the high place and establishes the new order.

Revolution at the beginning—changing the belly—this isauspicious, no blame.

The revolution's second—"changing the skin"—the gentleman is firm, correct—auspicious.

The revolution's third—"changing the practice"—there is danger, but no blame.

The revolution's fourth—"changing the faith"—remorse disappears—the gentleman is firm.

The revolution's fifth—"changing the great order"—the king commands—auspicious.

The revolution's top—"the highest revolution"—the cry of the tiger—the gentleman advances.
tuanshu_en:
Revolution: fire is below the lake—the flames rise and transform.
The nobleman announces the punishment in the high place and establishes the new order.
Revolution in the center—this is the time for change.
Revolution, correct and auspicious—heaven and earth transform, the four seasons complete.
Changing according to the time—this is the way of heaven.
vernacular_judgment_en: Revolution. Supreme success. It benefits to be correct and to see the great people.
vernacular_image_en: Lake above fire; the nobleman announces punishment in high places and establishes new order.
interpretation_en: A sign of revolution and transformation—this is a time for fundamental change. When conditions are ready, transformation brings success. One should act at the right moment for maximum effect.

name: 鼎
judgment: 鼎：元吉，亨。
tuanshu: 鼎，象也。以木巽火，亨饪也。圣人亨以享上帝，而大亨以养圣贤。巽而耳目聪明，柔进而上行，得中而应乎刚，是以元亨。
image:
木上有火，鼎；君子以正位凝命。
鼎颠趾，未悖也。利出否，以从贵也。
鼎有实，慎所之也。我仇有疾，终无尤也。
鼎耳革，失其义也。
覆公𫗧，信如何也。
鼎黄耳，中以为实也。
玉铉在上，刚柔节也。
interpretation: 木火相成、鼎器之象。问事大吉，三足鼎立、稳定有力。喻示承担重任、调和鼎鼐——但要养贤、选对人。
vernacular_judgment: 元吉，亨通。
vernacular_image: 木上有火，鼎，君子以正位凝命。
judgment_en: The Cauldron. Great success. The young phoenix appears.
image_en:
Fire above, wood below—this is The Cauldron;
The nobleman places it above and makes peace with the spirits.

The cauldron at the beginning—changing the belly—the gentleman uses it to change the order.

The cauldron's second—"the cauldron's ears are lifted"—the gentleman is firm—no blame.

The cauldron's third—"the cauldron's sides are removed"—the yellow share—the gentleman is correct—good fortune.

The cauldron's fourth—"the cauldron's legs are broken"—the duke's heart is overturned.

The cauldron's fifth—"the cauldron has yellow ears"—the gentleman is correct—auspicious.

The cauldron's top—"the cauldron is reinforced"—the jade ring is not perfect—auspicious.
tuanshu_en:
The Cauldron: wood enters the fire—cooking and transforming.
The nobleman places it above and makes peace with the spirits.
The cauldron is in the center—this is great success.
The cauldron's golden ears—the correct and auspicious.
What is incorrect is removed—what is correct is established.
vernacular_judgment_en: The Cauldron. Supreme success. It benefits to be correct.
vernacular_image_en: Fire above wood; the nobleman places it on high and makes peace with the spirits.
interpretation_en: A sign of the cauldron and transformation—refining and processing to create something valuable. This represents the process of cultivation and transformation that brings success.

name: 震
judgment: 震：亨。震来虩虩，笑言哑哑。震惊百里，不丧匕鬯。
tuanshu: 震，亨。震来虩虩，恐致福也。笑言哑哑，后有则也。震惊百里，惊远而惧迩也。出可以守宗庙社稷，以为祭主也。
image:
洊雷，震；君子以恐惧修省。
震来虩虩，恐致福也。笑言哑哑，后有则也。
震来厉，乘刚也。
震苏苏，位不当也。
震遂泥，未光也。
震往来厉，危行也。其事在中，大无丧也。
震索索，中未得也。虽凶无咎，畏邻戒也。
interpretation: 雷声叠叠、警醒之象。问事多有突发的震动或惊吓（坏消息、决策、惊险）。卦辞强调'恐致福'——震惊之后能反思修省则吉。
vernacular_judgment: 亨通，震来虩虩，笑言哑哑，震惊百里，不丧匕鬯。
vernacular_image: 洊雷震，君子以恐惧修省。
judgment_en: The Arousing. Success. Fear and trembling, then laughter. The messenger brings word of joy.
image_en:
Thunder above, thunder below—this is The Arousing;
The nobleman uses fright and fright to act.

The arousing at the beginning—fright and fright—auspicious.

The arousing's second—"danger is coming"—the gentleman sits and does not eat.

The arousing's third—"arousing and danger"—the gentleman goes out.

The arousing's fourth—"arousing and moving"—no place—is not settled.

The arousing's fifth—"danger is going"—no blame—the gentleman goes out.

The arousing's top—"arousing and fear"—misfortune—the gentleman advances and does not stop.
tuanshu_en:
The Arousing: thunder multiplies—this is the arousing and movement.
The nobleman uses fright to act and caution to protect.
Arousing in the center—fright and movement—the yang rises.
The arousing comes and frightens—all is in motion.
vernacular_judgment_en: The Arousing. Success. Thunder arrives and brings fright—startled, then laughing.
vernacular_image_en: Thunder above thunder; the nobleman uses fright to act.
interpretation_en: A sign of the arousing and shock—sudden changes and disturbances. This can be a time of sudden developments that require quick response. One should remain calm and use the energy of the moment.

name: 艮
judgment: 艮：艮其背，不获其身，行其庭，不见其人，无咎。
tuanshu: 艮，止也。时止则止，时行则行，动静不失其时，其道光明。艮其止，止其所也。上下敌应，不相与也。是以不获其身，行其庭不见其人，无咎也。
image:
兼山，艮；君子以思不出其位。
艮其趾，未失正也。
不拯其随，未退听也。
艮其限，危薰心也。
艮其身，止诸躬也。
艮其辅，以中正也。
敦艮之吉，以厚终也。
interpretation: 山止于地、止而不动之象。当前宜止、宜静、宜安住当下。问事多提示'止'——行止得所、动静合时方为正道。
vernacular_judgment: 艮其背，不获其身，行其庭，不见其人，无咎。
vernacular_image: 兼山艮，君子以思不出其位。
judgment_en: Keeping Still. Success. What is still cannot be moved. Contemplation of the still mountain.
image_en:
Mountain above, mountain below—this is Keeping Still;
The nobleman does not wander in the house.

Keeping still at the beginning—the foot is still—no place to go—remorse.

Keeping still in the second—keeping still in the middle—the gentleman is correct—no blame.

Keeping still in the third—the gentleman keeps still but is not upright—misfortune—no blame.

Keeping still in the fourth—"keeping still is the body"—no blame.

Keeping still in the five—"keeping still is the neck"—the gentleman is correct—auspicious.

Keeping still's top—"keeping still is happy"—auspicious—no blame.
tuanshu_en:
Keeping Still: mountain on mountain—this is the still and stop.
The nobleman does not wander in the house—to wander brings sorrow.
Keeping still in the center—the gentleman keeps still and does not forget danger.
Keeping still is stillness in the correct position.
vernacular_judgment_en: Keeping Still. Success. The gentleman keeps still and does not wander in the house.
vernacular_image_en: Mountain above mountain; the nobleman does not wander within the house.
interpretation_en: A sign of stillness and stopping—when one must stop and remain still. This is a time for inner reflection and conservation of energy. The key is knowing when to act and when to be still.

name: 渐
judgment: 渐：女归吉，利贞。
tuanshu: 渐之进也，女归吉也。进得位，往有功也。进以正，可以正邦也。其位刚，得中也。止而巽，动不穷也。
image:
山上有木，渐；君子以居贤德，善俗。
小子之厉，义无咎也。
饮食衎衎，不素饱也。
夫征不复，离群丑也。妇孕不育，失其道也。利用御寇，顺相保也。
或得其桷，顺以巽也。
终莫之胜，吉；得所愿也。
其羽可用为仪，吉；不可乱也。
interpretation: 山上有木、渐生之象。问事大吉，进展虽慢但稳健有序。喻示如女子出嫁须待礼成——凡事不可急躁，循序渐进。
vernacular_judgment: 女归吉，利贞。
vernacular_image: 山上有木，渐，君子以居贤德善俗。
judgment_en: Development. Success. The woman returns to her home. It is beneficial to have somewhere to go.
image_en:
Wind above, mountain below—this is Development;
The nobleman uses proper conduct to influence others.

Development at the beginning—wild geese approach the cliff—auspicious—the gentleman does not forget to retreat.

The development's second—"the goose approaches the hill"—the gentleman uses this to consolidate—no blame.

The development's third—"the goose approaches the tree"—the gentleman obtains a follower.

The development's fourth—"the goose approaches the wood"—the gentleman obtains the guests—remorse disappears.

The development's fifth—"the goose approaches the cloud"—the gentleman obtains the guests—auspicious.

The development's top—"the goose approaches the feather"—the gentleman advances in white.
tuanshu_en:
Development: the wind moves above the mountain—gradual advance.
The nobleman uses proper conduct to influence and attract.
Development in the center—correct and auspicious.
The goose gradually advances—the gentleman uses this to move forward.
vernacular_judgment_en: Development. Success. It benefits to be correct; it benefits to have somewhere to go.
vernacular_image_en: Wind above mountain; the nobleman uses proper conduct to influence others.
interpretation_en: A sign of gradual development and advancement—steady progress toward goals. This is the time for careful, patient advancement. One should advance methodically and maintain proper conduct.

name: 归妹
judgment: 归妹：征凶，无攸利。
tuanshu: 归妹，天地之大义也。天地不交，而万物不兴，归妹人之终始也。说以动，所归妹也。征凶，位不当也。无攸利，柔乘刚也。
image:
泽上有雷，归妹；君子以永终知敝。
归妹以娣，以恒也。跛能履吉，相承也。
利幽人之贞，未变常也。
归妹以须，未当也。
愆期之志，有待而行也。
帝乙归妹，不如其娣之袂良也。其位在中，以贵行也。
interpretation: 雷泽相归、少女从长男之象。问感情、合作虽可成但需慎始。归妹提示'归'的本质——归宿定则安，但所归不当则困。
vernacular_judgment: 征凶，无攸利。
vernacular_image: 泽上有雷，归妹，君子以永终知敝。
judgment_en: The Marrying Maiden. Misfortune. Nothing is beneficial.
image_en:
Lake above, thunder below—this is The Marrying Maiden;
The nobleman uses the proper timing to act.

The marrying at the beginning—"the maiden is not married"—this isauspicious, no blame.

The marrying's second—"the one-eyed can see"—the gentleman can use it—no blame.

The marrying's third—"the maiden is given in marriage"—the man takes a concubine—the lady's proper timing is not.

The marrying's fourth—"marrying is late"—the gentleman is delayed.

The marrying's five—"the emperor gives the lady"—the lady is like a piece of silk—the king marries the lady from the west.

The marrying's top—"the woman carries the basket"—there is no fruit—the man spears the goose.
tuanshu_en:
The Marrying Maiden: thunder moves below the lake—the woman follows the man.
The nobleman uses the proper timing to act.
Marrying in the center—the woman is modest and obedient.
What is not timely leads to regret—acting at the right time brings success.
vernacular_judgment_en: The Marrying Maiden. It benefits to be correct. The woman carries the basket—there is no fruit.
vernacular_image_en: Lake above thunder; the nobleman uses proper timing to act.
interpretation_en: A sign of the marrying maiden and yielding—this represents the proper relationship between opposites. One should understand the proper timing and sequence of events for success.

name: 丰
judgment: 丰：亨。王假之，勿忧，宜日中。
tuanshu: 丰，大也。明以动，故丰。王假之，尚大也。勿忧宜日中，宜照天下也。日中则昃，月盈则食，天地盈虚，与时消息，而况于人乎？况于鬼神乎？
image:
雷电皆至，丰；君子以折狱致刑。
虽旬无咎，过旬灾也。
有孚发若，信以发志也。
丰其沛，不可大事也。折其右肱，终不可用也。
丰其蔀，位不当也。日中见斗，幽不明也。遇其夷主，吉；行也。
六五之吉，有庆也。
丰其屋，天际翔也。窥其户，阒其无人，自藏也。
interpretation: 雷火相照、盛大之象。当前规模大、势头旺。问事虽成但要警惕'丰'的阴影——盛大极易转为衰落，明者当预为之备。
vernacular_judgment: 亨通，王假之，勿忧，宜日中。
vernacular_image: 雷电皆至，丰，君子以折狱致刑。
judgment_en: Abundance. Great success. It is beneficial to have somewhere to go.
image_en:
Fire above, thunder below—this is Abundance;
The nobleman judges matters and establishes punishments.

Abundance at the beginning—abundance and decline—the gentleman knows the two ends.

The abundance's second—"abundance covers the east and west"—the gentleman is correct—auspicious.

The abundance's third—"abundance is lost in the middle"—the gentleman is correct—auspicious—the gentleman uses the bright.

The abundance's four—"abundance is white"—the gentleman obtains the guests—the king is not envious.

The abundance's five—"there are connections"—the gentleman is correct—auspicious.

The abundance's top—"abundance is in the house"—the gentleman is covered—no blame.
tuanshu_en:
Abundance: thunder and fire are bright—this is abundance and brightness.
The nobleman judges matters and establishes punishments.
Abundance in the center—correct and auspicious.
Abundance cannot be preserved forever—this is the way of heaven.
vernacular_judgment_en: Abundance. The great person is correct—auspicious. The east and west are covered.
vernacular_image_en: Fire above thunder; the nobleman judges matters and establishes punishments.
interpretation_en: A sign of abundance and fullness—when everything reaches its peak. This is a time of great activity and achievement. However, one should remember that abundance does not last forever.

name: 旅
judgment: 旅：小亨，旅贞吉。
tuanshu: 旅，小亨，柔得中乎外，而顺乎刚，止而丽乎明，是以小亨，旅贞吉也。旅之时义大矣哉！
image:
山上有火，旅；君子以明慎用刑，而不留狱。
旅琐琐，志穷灾也。
得童仆贞，终无尤也。
旅焚其次，亦以伤矣。以旅与下，其义丧也。
旅于处，未得位也。得其资斧，心未快也。
终以誉命，上逮也。
以旅在上，其义焚也。丧牛于易，终莫之闻也。
interpretation: 火山相依、客旅之象。问出行、迁徙、外派、合作皆有所指。客居者宜柔顺、守小、不可张扬（旅人强梁必败）。
vernacular_judgment: 小亨，旅贞吉。
vernacular_image: 山上有火，旅，君子以明慎用刑而不留狱。
judgment_en: The Wanderer. Success. It is beneficial to have somewhere to go. Small matters bring good fortune.
image_en:
Fire above, mountain below—this is The Wanderer;
The nobleman uses clearness and brightness to influence others.

The wanderer's beginning—the wanderer at the beginning—this isauspicious—the gentleman conquers.

The wanderer's second—"the wanderer in the small"—the gentleman obtains the cart—do not use the person.

The wanderer's third—"the wanderer burns the luggage"—the gentleman sits and loses it.

The wanderer's fourth—"the wanderer is at the rest"—the gentleman obtains the axe—not my heart's desire.

The wanderer's five—"the wanderer draws the bow"—the gentleman is firm—auspicious.

The wanderer's top—"the bird burns the nest"—the gentleman first laughs, then cries.
tuanshu_en:
The Wanderer: fire is above, mountain is below—bright yet stopping.
The nobleman uses clearness and brightness to influence others.
Wanderer in the center—this is the wanderer's correctness.
The wanderer who is correct is not destroyed—the way of heaven protects.
vernacular_judgment_en: The Wanderer. Success. It benefits to be correct; it benefits to have somewhere to go.
vernacular_image_en: Fire above mountain; the nobleman uses clarity and brightness to influence others.
interpretation_en: A sign of the wanderer and travel—being away from home in foreign lands. This can be a time of exile or journey. One should maintain integrity while wandering and stay connected to one's roots.

name: 巽
judgment: 巽：小亨。利有攸往。利见大人。
tuanshu: 重巽以申命，刚巽乎中正而志行。柔皆顺乎刚，是以小亨，利有攸往，利见大人。
image:
随风，巽；君子以申命行事。
进退，志疑也。利武人之贞，志治也。
纷若之吉，得中也。
频巽之吝，志穷也。
田获三品，有功也。
九五之吉，位正中也。
巽在床下，上穷也。丧其资斧，正乎凶也。
interpretation: 风行天下、无所不入之象。问事以柔顺渗透为法——不与硬碰，迂回达成。问合作、说服、教育最合适。
vernacular_judgment: 小亨，利有攸往，利见大人。
vernacular_image: 随风巽，君子以申命行事。
judgment_en: The Gentle. Success. It is beneficial to have somewhere to go. Propitious omens.
image_en:
Wind above, wind below—this is The Gentle;
The nobleman uses the many to act.

The gentle at the beginning—going forward in the gentle—advancing isauspicious.

The gentle's second—"the gentle is in the middle"—the gentleman obtains the followers.

The gentle's third—"repenting over the gentle"—remorse disappears—the gentleman obtains the followers.

The gentle's fourth—"the gentle hunts the three"—the gentleman obtains the big catch.

The gentle's five—"the gentle has no place"—the gentleman obtains the guests.

The gentle's top—"the gentle in the wings"—the gentleman advances but does not retreat.
tuanshu_en:
The Gentle: wind follows wind—this is the gentle movement.
The nobleman uses the many to act—the small and the great are both served.
Gentle in the center—the gentleman obtains followers.
Those who advance with gentleness succeed—what can oppose?
vernacular_judgment_en: The Gentle. Success. It benefits to have somewhere to go; it benefits to see great people.
vernacular_image_en: Wind above wind; the nobleman uses gentle persistence to accomplish tasks.
interpretation_en: A sign of gentleness and penetration—soft power that gradually overcomes obstacles. This is a time for subtle, persistent effort that eventually achieves the goal through gentle strength.

name: 兑
judgment: 兑：亨。利贞。
tuanshu: 兑，说也。刚中而柔外，说以利贞，是以顺乎天，而应乎人。说以先民，民忘其劳；说以犯难，民忘其死；说之大，民劝矣哉！
image:
丽泽，兑；君子以朋友讲习。
和兑之吉，行未疑也。
孚兑之吉，信志也。
来兑之凶，位不当也。
九四之喜，有庆也。
孚于剥，位正当也。
interpretation: 两泽相依、悦之象。问事多吉，但核心在'口'——言语和悦则人和，言语伤人则祸至。问感情、合作、口才皆利。
vernacular_judgment: 亨通，利贞。
vernacular_image: 丽泽兑，君子以朋友讲习。
judgment_en: The Joyous. Success. Perseverance brings good fortune. Sincerity brings harmony.
image_en:
Lake above, lake below—this is The Joyous;
The nobleman uses pleasure to attract the people.

The joyous at the beginning—the joyous does not end—auspicious.

The joyous' second—"the joyous is truthful"—good fortune, no blame.

The joyous' third—"the joyous brings sorrow"—remorse—but no blame.

The joyous' fourth—"the joyous is not at rest"—the heart is not decided—misfortune.

The joyous' five—"the joyous destroys"—danger—but there is no blame.

The joyous' top—"the joyous stops"—misfortune.
tuanshu_en:
The Joyous: lake upon lake—this is the joyous increase.
The nobleman uses pleasure to attract the people.
Joyous in the center—this is the gentleman's correctness.
What is joyful is not the same as what is excessive—the gentleman knows the limit.
vernacular_judgment_en: The Joyous. Success. It benefits to be correct; it benefits to have somewhere to go.
vernacular_image_en: Lake above lake; the nobleman uses pleasure to attract the people.
interpretation_en: A sign of joy and pleasure—happiness and contentment. This is a time of celebration and enjoyment. However, one should maintain balance and not overindulge.

name: 涣
judgment: 涣：亨。王假有庙，利涉大川，利贞。
tuanshu: 涣，亨。刚来而不穷，柔得位乎外而上同。王假有庙，王乃在中也。利涉大川，乘木有功也。
image: 风行水上，涣；先王以享于帝立庙。
interpretation: 风行水上、涣散之象。当前人心不齐、各自为政。问事多有离散之兆——需有核心凝聚（先王以享祀聚人心），方能化散为聚。
vernacular_judgment: 亨通，王假有庙，利涉大川，利贞。
vernacular_image: 风行水上，涣，先王以享于帝立庙。
judgment_en: Dispersion. Success. It is beneficial to have somewhere to go. The king builds walls.
image_en:
Wind above, water below—this is Dispersion;
The nobleman announces the offering and purifies the people.

Dispersion at the beginning—"dispersion and going"—auspicious, no blame.

The dispersion's second—"the dispersion is in the middle"—the gentleman uses this to consolidate.

The dispersion's third—"the dispersion does not have a root"—the gentleman advances—no blame.

The dispersion's four—"the dispersion is above"—the gentleman obtains the guests.

The dispersion's five—"the dispersion goes out"—the king presents the offering—the gentleman is correct.

The dispersion's top—"the dispersion is broken"—no help from others—misfortune.
tuanshu_en:
Dispersion: wind moves over water—this is the dispersion and scattering.
The nobleman announces the offering and purifies the people.
Dispersion in the center—correct and auspicious.
Dispersion dissolves obstruction—those who are correct succeed.
vernacular_judgment_en: Dispersion. Success. It benefits to be correct; in seven days one obtains the result.
vernacular_image_en: Wind above water; the nobleman announces offerings and purifies the people.
interpretation_en: A sign of dispersion and dissolution—when things break apart and scatter. This can be a time of clearing away obstacles and preparing for new beginnings. One should use this time for purification and renewal.

name: 节
judgment: 节：亨。苦节不可贞。
tuanshu: 节，亨，刚柔分，而刚得中。苦节不可贞，其道穷也。说以行险，当位以节，中正以通。天地节而四时成，节以制度，不伤财，不害民。
image:
泽上有水，节；君子以制数度，议德行。
不出户庭，知通塞也。
不出门庭，失时极也。
不节之嗟，又谁咎也。
安节之亨，承上道也。
甘节之吉，居位中也。
苦节贞凶，其道穷也。
interpretation: 泽上有水、节制之象。问事大吉，凡事有度方能亨通。过度则溢、不及则枯——把握尺度是关键。
vernacular_judgment: 亨通，苦节不可贞。
vernacular_image: 泽上有水，节，君子以制数度，议德行。
judgment_en: Limitation. Success. Perseverance brings good fortune. Know the limitation.
image_en:
Water above, lake below—this is Limitation;
The nobleman establishes the proper order and distinguishes the boundaries.

Limitation at the beginning—not leaving the gate—remorse.

The limitation's second—not leaving the center—correct—the gentleman is firm—good fortune.

The limitation's third—"limited and difficult"—the gentleman is correct—remorse disappears.

The limitation's four—"the limitation is the exit"—the gentleman obtains the guests—auspicious.

The limitation's five—"the limitation is the offering"—the king presents the offering—the gentleman is correct—auspicious.

The limitation's top—"the limitation is bitter"—the limitation cannot be restricted—misfortune.
tuanshu_en:
Limitation: water is above the lake—there is a limit to the lake.
The nobleman establishes the proper order and distinguishes boundaries.
Limitation in the center—correct and auspicious.
Limitation must have its proper measure—the gentleman knows the limit.
vernacular_judgment_en: Limitation. Success. The limitation cannot be restricted—remorse in the end.
vernacular_image_en: Water above lake; the nobleman establishes proper order and distinguishes boundaries.
interpretation_en: A sign of limitation and restraint—establishing boundaries and limits. This is a time for self-discipline and setting proper limits. Knowing when to say no is essential for success.

name: 中孚
judgment: 中孚：豚鱼吉，利涉大川，利贞。
tuanshu: 中孚，柔在内而刚得中。说而巽，孚，乃化邦也。豚鱼吉，信及豚鱼也。利涉大川，乘木舟虚也。中孚以利贞，乃应乎天也。
image: 泽上有风，中孚；君子以议狱缓死。
interpretation: 泽上有风、中心诚信之象。问事大吉——至诚感通、无物不化。问感情、合作、事业皆以'诚'为根本，诚则无敌。
vernacular_judgment: 豚鱼吉，利涉大川，利贞。
vernacular_image: 泽上有风，中孚，君子以议狱缓死。
judgment_en: Inner Truth. Success. It is beneficial to cross the great river. The crane calls in the shade.
image_en:
Wind above, lake below—this is Truthfulness;
The nobleman establishes trust in the center and corrects the people.

The truthfulness at the beginning—inner truth—auspicious—no blame—the gentleman is correct.

The truthfulness's second—"the crane cries in the clouds"—the son understands—good fortune—the gentleman has something to depend upon.

The truthfulness's third—"the toad beats the drum"—the son obtains the guests—the lady is not given to.

The truthfulness's four—"the horse is stopped"—the gentleman obtains the guests—the gentleman does not advance.

The truthfulness's five—"the truthful is united"—the gentleman is correct—auspicious.

The truthfulness's top—"the crane cries in the clouds"—the son understands—the gentleman has something to be ashamed of.
tuanshu_en:
Truthfulness: wind moves above the lake—the truth is within.
The nobleman establishes trust in the center and corrects the people.
Truthfulness in the center—this is the correct way.
Those who are truthful are trusted—how could they be deceived?
vernacular_judgment_en: Truthfulness. Success. In the middle, good fortune; the crane in the clouds—the son understands.
vernacular_image_en: Wind above lake; the nobleman establishes trust in the center and corrects the people.
interpretation_en: A sign of truthfulness and inner sincerity—genuine integrity that inspires trust. This is a time when sincerity and honest communication bring success. One should maintain inner truth.

name: 小过
judgment: 小过：亨。利贞。可小事，不可大事。飞鸟遗之音，不宜上宜下，大吉。
tuanshu: 小过，小者过而亨也。过以利贞，与时行也。柔得中，是以小事吉也。刚失位而不中，是以不可大事也。有飞鸟之象焉，有飞鸟遗之音，不宜上宜下，大吉；上逆而下顺也。
image:
山上有雷，小过；君子以行过乎恭，丧过乎哀，用过乎俭。
飞鸟以凶，不可如何也。
不及其君，臣不可过也。
从或戕之，凶如何也。
弗过遇之，位不当也。往厉必戒，终不可长也。
密云不雨，已上也。
弗遇过之，已亢也。
interpretation: 飞鸟遗音、过犹不及之象。当前宜小事可为、大事不可。问事多涉小过——小事可亨，大事需静守。
vernacular_judgment: 亨通，利贞，可小事，不可大事，飞鸟遗之音，不宜上宜下，大吉。
vernacular_image: 山上有雷，小过，君子以行过乎恭。
judgment_en: Small Exceeding. It is beneficial to have somewhere to go. Do not act on great matters.
image_en:
Thunder above, mountain below—this is Small Excess;
The nobleman uses great smallness to transform and influence others.

The small excess at the beginning—"the bird flies"—misfortune—do not try to ascend.

The small excess's second—"the excess is passed"—the gentleman is correct—good fortune—do not try to change.

The small excess's third—"the small excess goes to the great"—the gentleman obtains the guests—the gentleman uses the excess.

The small excess's fourth—"the small excess passes"—no blame—the gentleman is firm—good fortune.

The small excess's five—"the excess is like a goose"—the gentleman obtains the guests—auspicious—the king presents the offering.

The small excess's top—"the excess is like flying birds"—misfortune—do not try to ascend.
tuanshu_en:
Small Excess: thunder is above the mountain—movement and stillness.
The nobleman uses great smallness to transform and influence others.
Small excess in the center—correct and auspicious.
The small bird should not try to fly—what is excess cannot be changed.
The way of heaven diminishes what is full and increases what is empty.
vernacular_judgment_en: Small Excess. Success. It benefits to be correct; do not try to change what is small.
vernacular_image_en: Thunder above mountain; the nobleman uses great attention to detail to transform and influence others.
interpretation_en: A sign of small excess—when details become too important. This is a time to be careful about focusing on minor things. One should maintain proper balance and not overdo it.

name: 既济
judgment: 既济：亨小。利贞。初吉终乱。
tuanshu: 既济，亨，小者亨也。利贞，刚柔正而位当也。初吉，柔得中也。终止则乱，其道穷也。
image:
水在火上，既济；君子以思患而豫防之。
曳其轮，义无咎也。
七日得，以中道也。
三年克之，惫也。
终日戒，有所疑也。
东邻杀牛，不如西邻之时也；实受其福，吉大来也。
濡其首厉，何可久也。
interpretation: 水火相交、已经完成之象。问事初吉，但需慎终——已成则易骄，骄则败。既济提示居安思危、守成之难甚于创业。
vernacular_judgment: 亨通，小利贞，初吉终乱。
vernacular_image: 水在火上，既济，君子以思患而预防之。
judgment_en: After Completion. Success. Small fortune at the end of crossing.
image_en:
Water above, fire below—this is After Completion;
The nobleman considers the beginning and the end to know the result.

After completion at the beginning—the horse's tail—no blame—the gentleman is correct.

The after completion's second—"the woman loses the curtain"—do not chase—the gentleman is correct.

The after completion's third—"the after completion is not completed"—the gentleman is firm—misfortune.

The after completion's four—"the skin is changed"—the gentleman uses the wine.

The after completion's five—"the neighbor with the knife"—the king presents the offering—the gentleman is correct.

The after completion's top—"the head is immersed"—misfortune.
tuanshu_en:
After Completion: water is above fire—the fire is not overcome.
The nobleman considers the beginning and the end to know the result.
After completion in the center—correct and auspicious.
What is not correct at the beginning leads to misfortune at the end.
Those who are correct succeed—how could they be lost?
vernacular_judgment_en: After Completion. Success. The horse's tail—no blame. The woman loses the curtain—do not chase.
vernacular_image_en: Water above fire; the nobleman considers the beginning and the end to know the outcome.
interpretation_en: A sign of after completion—when things appear finished but require continued vigilance. This is a time to review and ensure that everything is truly complete. One should not become careless after apparent success.

name: 未济
judgment: 未济：亨。小狐汔济，濡其尾，无攸利。
tuanshu: 未济，亨；柔得中也。小狐汔济，未出中也。濡其尾，无攸利；不续终也。虽不当位，刚柔应也。
image:
火在水上，未济；君子以慎辨物居方。
濡其尾，亦不知极也。
九二贞吉，中以行正也。
未济征凶，位不当也。
贞吉悔亡，志行也。
君子之光，其晖吉也。
饮酒濡首，亦不知节也。
interpretation: 水火不交、尚未完成之象。问事未完但有希望——小狐狸渡河快到岸但尾巴仍湿。提示坚持到最后一步，转机就在前方。
vernacular_judgment: 亨通，小狐汔济，濡其尾，无攸利。
vernacular_image: 火在水上，未济，君子以慎辨物居方。
judgment_en: Before Completion. Success. The great person crosses the great river. Small fox gets stuck.
image_en:
Fire above, water below—this is Before Completion;
The nobleman considers the beginning to know the end.

The before completion at the beginning—the gentleman drinks wine—no blame.

The before completion's second—"the woman loses the curtain"—chase—the gentleman is correct—remorse disappears.

The before completion's third—"the before completion is not completed"—the gentleman is firm—misfortune—the gentleman advances.

The before completion's fourth—"the before completion is like a dream"—the gentleman is not drunk—the gentleman is correct.

The before completion's five—"the king presents the offering"—the gentleman is correct—auspicious.

The before completion's top—"the drunk is drenched"—the gentleman is correct—misfortune.
tuanshu_en:
Before Completion: fire is above water—the fire and water do not rest.
The nobleman considers the beginning to know the end.
Before completion in the center—the lady is not given to—this is correct.
What is not correct at the end cannot be completed—what is completed cannot be corrected.
vernacular_judgment_en: Before Completion. Success. The woman loses the curtain—chase; the gentleman is correct.
vernacular_image_en: Fire above water; the nobleman considers the beginning to know the outcome.
interpretation_en: A sign of before completion—when things are almost but not quite finished. This is the critical time before achievement. One must be careful and thorough to complete successfully. The final push determines success or failure.
